## [00:00 - 02:58]

Okay, so if we're looking at creative, so the images, here are typically the four angles that we use inside of the agency to work with these types of niches, these funnels. So one of the pillars, if you will call it a pillar, is called pattern interrupt. You'll see here it's when you're just basically using an image, it's kind of irrelevant. The main aim is that it stops the thumb in the newsfeed, so it's usually contrasting images or really unusual photos, and the aim is that you're gonna stop the thumb long enough that they read your headline, and if you're targeting is any good, it should be the right audience. It pulls them into your copy and therefore into your funnel. The second pillar is relevance. So for example, if you're targeting coaches or if your coach that you're working with is targeting doctors, you would have images of doctors or surgeons. Now this pillar isn't always my favorite because it can tend to look very stock image-like, as you can see in this example here. However, if you find appropriate images, it can work really well. So for example, if your coach or your client is helping plumber's build up their business, and you have a photo of a toilet or plumbing, you know, kit, then that can work really, really well, right? Because it's relevant to the audience. The third pillar is authenticity. So this is like iPhone shots. This is basically photos, behind-the-scenes photos, or just photos that you've taken out and about. This pillar works really well because they don't look like ads, right? They just look like normal newsfeed posts that a page or a friend would post, and so it tends to stop the thumb if it's a captivating photo. And then finally, the last pillar is images of you or your client. So you can see here, those are examples of photos that I've been using inside some of my ads. It is my face. It works well because people recognize me, the people that I'm targeting recognize me. And also it builds that trust because it is using my face. So it's creating that human relationship. So those are typically the four pillars that work well in this industry. Now, what I would recommend is every ad set should have at least three ads that you test out. And so what I would recommend is you almost test out one of each of those pillars. So you don't have to do all four. Ideally, you would have four ads and you're testing out one from each. But let's say you've got three ads, one of the images is a pattern interrupt, one of them is an authentic, and then one of them is an image of your client or yourself or an image that's relevant to that target audience. So this is a bit of a framework around how we choose creative for our clients ads.
